user_query:
Hey team! Before we record our Sports & Popcorn segment, can you fact-check the pop-culture crossover claims in input/claims.jsonl against our local references and prep materials for the meeting?

What I need:
1) Implement and run a command-line script (any language is fine) that reads:
   - input/claims.jsonl (JSONL claims)
   - input/facts.json (canonical reference facts)
   - input/corrections.csv (known misattributions)
   and writes a machine-readable report to verification/verification_report.json.

Classification rules (apply in this order):
- verified: At least one record in input/facts.json matches all provided claim fields: subject, claim_type, title, and year (if year is not null).
- ambiguous: The claim has year null/empty and there are two or more records in input/facts.json matching the same subject, claim_type, and title but with different years.
- disputed: No verifying fact is found, and either (a) input/corrections.csv has a row matching subject, claim_type, wrong_title, and wrong_year (if the claim year is null, treat wrong_year as matching any year), or (b) input/facts.json contains a record with the same title and year but a different subject than the claim.
- not_found: None of the above conditions apply.

Report requirements (write to verification/verification_report.json):
- JSON array where each element includes at least:
  - claim_id (from the claims file)
  - status (one of: verified, ambiguous, disputed, not_found)
  - matched_fact_ids (array of fact IDs from input/facts.json that support or clarify the status; include all matches for verified/ambiguous, or empty if none)
  - conflicting_fact_ids (array of fact IDs from input/facts.json that explicitly contradict a claim for the disputed case, if applicable; otherwise empty)
  - correction_note (string; include the note from input/corrections.csv if a matching correction is found, else empty)
  - rationale (short free-text note explaining which rule triggered the status and why)

2) Compose a concise status update at docs/status_update.md that includes:
   - The exact command you ran to produce verification/verification_report.json.
   - The total number of claims processed and a count by status (verified, ambiguous, disputed, not_found).
   - A short summary highlighting anything we should be excited or careful about (keep it brief and factual).

3) Prepare meeting-ready notes with action items at docs/meeting_notes.md:
   - For each claim with status disputed or not_found, add a bullet with: claim_id, a one-line restatement of the claim, the next step (e.g., “Replace with: <correction_note>” if available or “Research needed”), and an owner (“fact-check”) with due date “before recording.”
   - Include a brief opening line (one sentence) to set context for the team.

Reproducibility: The outputs must be fully derived from the provided input files and the rules above. Please create the verification/ and docs/ directories if they don’t exist and keep paths exactly as specified.

Inputs are in the input/ directory. Outputs must be:
- verification/verification_report.json
- docs/status_update.md
- docs/meeting_notes.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
